title: QUIC RFC 9002 PTO Timer Discipline Work Item
summary: Keep the RFC 9002 PTO timer discipline slice traceable while the runtime-owned recovery state arms PTO, restarts it on the supported transitions, and applies validation-aware gating before handshake confirmation.
planned_changes:
- Reuse the packet-space recovery ledger as the single PTO timer source of truth instead of introducing a parallel timer store.
- Reset PTO backoff on ack-eliciting send, acknowledgment, and key discard while preserving the current zero-backoff floor.
- Gate pre-confirmation client and server PTO arming on actual validation state rather than on test-only assumptions.
- Keep PTO expiration as scheduler state only, leaving packet-loss marking, probe composition, retry reset, and migration switching to separate slices.
verification_plan: Run the targeted RFC 9002 requirement-home tests and inspect the recovery-timing, send-runtime, and validation seams that govern PTO arming, restart, backoff reset, and pre-confirmation gating.
out_of_scope: Probe composition or packet selection after PTO expiry.
Treating PTO expiry as a packet-loss declaration.
completion_notes: The PTO timer discipline slice is proven by the targeted requirement-home tests and the runtime helper seams already present in the repository. PTO expiry remains scheduler state only, and the probe, retry, and migration follow-ons stay separate.
